name: behavioral-steering-lives-in-skills-not-langgraph
tags: task framework, execution philosophy, skills vs langgraph, task-grooming, task-introspection, task-implementation, reflection loop, abandoned epic
body:
Epic 2f0fb672 (standing Execution Contract + per-tool Reflection loop, mechanical LangGraph/PostToolUse wiring) was abandoned 2026-07-05 — only its first prototype (task:380140fa, static Execution Contract injection at activation) shipped and stays.

Why: per-tool Reflection injection and a mechanical loop-detector would have required new PostToolUse/session_graph wiring for a problem that's really about behavioral steering, not state machinery. Decision: this class of guidance belongs in skill prose (improved /task-grooming, /task-introspection, and a new /task-implementation skill with behavioral guidelines), not in the LangGraph session design.

How to apply: future ideas for steering agent behavior mid-task (anti-drift, anti-looping, confidence checks, "when to stop exploring") should default to skill-prose additions first. Only reach for LangGraph/checkpoint/hook wiring if the mechanism genuinely requires cross-turn state that skill instructions can't carry on their own (the Execution Contract qualified because it needed to be injected identically every turn from the checkpoint; a reflection prompt doesn't need that).
